authoritative_sources.title: Reliability Explainer
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC is the federal reliability regulator and its explainer clarifies the boundary between reliability oversight and state or utility-local operational authority, which keeps this lane from over-claiming approval rights.
used_for: separating restoration execution support from formal regulator, market, or adequacy authority
defining why final public or regulatory commitments remain outside the lane

authoritative_sources.title: United States Electricity Industry Primer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOE provides a vendor-neutral federal overview of the U.S. electricity sector and explicitly describes prestorm preparation, mutual assistance, damage assessment, and the typical restoration process used by utilities.
used_for: named restoration stages from damage assessment through feeder and customer restoration
scenario design for normal restoration progression

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.269 - Electric power generation, transmission, and distribution
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA supplies the controlling federal worker-safety baseline for utility restoration work and therefore defines non-delegable safety boundaries for energized equipment, line-clearance work, and job-briefing-dependent actions.
used_for: refusal triggers for switching and re-energization authority requests
keeping safety signoff outside the lane
bounding restoration actions that depend on energized-work judgment

authoritative_sources.title: Glossary of Terms Used in NERC Reliability Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC's glossary is the official vocabulary source for reliability-related utility roles and system terms that keep this lane grounded in electric-power terminology rather than generic field-service language.
used_for: artifact and role naming in outage-restoration outputs
distinguishing restoration execution from adjacent dispatch, compliance, and reliability roles

authoritative_sources.title: General Order 166: Standards for Operation, Reliability, and Safety During Emergencies and Disasters
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GO 166 is a detailed public utility-regulator order covering emergency plans, damage assessment, restoration-priority establishment, estimated restoration times, personnel redeployment, and annual reporting for major outages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: damage assessment, ETR handling, and reporting
used_for: domain failure modes when restoration claims outpace assessed conditions

authoritative_sources.title: 16 NYCRR Part 105 Electric Utility Emergency Plans
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York State Department of Public Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DPS-hosted emergency-plan appendix reproduces Part 105 requirements for service restoration procedures, customer contacts, mutual assistance agreements, and commission review of restoration performance.
used_for: blocked-state handling when emergency-plan prerequisites are absent

authoritative_sources.title: Mutual Aid Playbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APPA is a recognized public-power trade body and its playbook provides concrete mutual-aid roles, coordination meetings, resource-information forms, and crew-coordination artifacts used in restoration mobilizations.
used_for: routing when outside crews are not actually ready for restoration work

workflow_stages.name: Accepted outage framing and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Interpret the accepted outage incident in utility terms, confirm feeder or device scope, and verify that outage, switching, and priority records exist before state progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Damage assessment and hazard control
workflow_stages.description: Use patrol, damage assessment, hazard, and clearance records to decide whether restoration work can proceed, must hold, or requires safety or engineering escalation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Restoration sequencing and crew mobilization
workflow_stages.description: Track restoration priority, critical-customer context, feeder or lateral sequencing, crew readiness, mutual aid, and materials dependencies without improvising unsupported priorities.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and low-confidence escalation
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing records, conflicting OMS and field state, jurisdiction conflicts, and authority gaps through explicit exception states and escalation packaging.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and restoration closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture restoration closeout evidence, verify that outage state is consistent across records, and only generate completion outputs when evidence supports closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: utility emergency response plan and restoration procedures
OMS outage incident and customer-impact records
feeder, device, and GIS topology references
switching orders, clearance references, hold cards, and control-room logs
damage assessment forms, patrol notes, hazard reports, and field photos
critical-customer or essential-facility priority lists and ETR status notes
mutual-aid resource-information forms, crew-coordination sheets, and staging details
restoration closeout packet with patrol verification and completion confirmation
systems_of_record: ADMS, DMS, or SCADA state records
switching log or control-room log system
crew scheduling and workforce management system
customer-contact, call-center, or outage-status communication system

allowed: Assess restoration lifecycle state and evidence sufficiency for accepted outage work.
Track outage, switching, crew, mutual-aid, and customer-priority dependencies and package next-step summaries.
Generate execution status, exception, and completion records when they remain recommendation-only and evidence-backed.
escalate: Requests requiring switching, re-energization, safety signoff, engineered temporary repair approval, or public-regulatory commitment.
Conflicting state emergency-plan rules, tenant communication policy, or source records that materially change restoration handling.
Missing critical-customer, damage-assessment, or mutual-aid records that would change the restoration priority or readiness answer.
refuse: Claims that restoration is complete without switching closeout, field verification, or customer-restoration evidence.
Requests to authorize energized work, live switching, or return-to-service decisions.
Requests that treat outage restoration as generic work-order closure and omit outage-specific records or customer-impact context.

domain_failure_modes: generic execution output that ignores feeder topology, switching state, damage assessment, or customer-impact records
restoration completion claims made without synchronized outage, switching, and field closeout evidence
unsupported ETR or restoration-priority decisions made without approved tenant policy or critical-customer records
overreach into switching, safety, engineering, or external public-communications authority
mutual-aid readiness claims made without actual crew, staging, and resource-information artifacts

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific major-outage and ETR communication rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State utility rules and tenant communication policy differ on when and how restoration estimates may be published, so runtime must retrieve the governing tenant procedure before high-confidence external-status packaging.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Mutual-aid coordination model by utility ownership type
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public-power, cooperative, and investor-owned utilities use materially different mutual-aid coordination structures, so runtime must know the tenant model before assuming state-coordinator or utility-to-utility workflows.

notes: Systems of record are an inference from the named outage, switching, customer-contact, and mutual-aid artifacts in the sources; exact platform names remain tenant-specific runtime facts.

The lane is materially different from generic execution because utility outage restoration is governed by topology, damage assessment, public-safety hazards, customer-priority rules, mutual aid, and evidence-backed closeout.
